heroHeadline: Day 34: O3 Succession Consolidation Ongoing; Mexico Deploys 100,000 for FIFA World Cup Security
heroSubtitle: Day 34 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa. Juan Carlos Valencia González ('O3') continues consolidating CJNG leadership under a collective council structure, confirmed by U.S. NCTC assessment, while El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) remains a rival power center at large. President Sheinbaum has ordered a 100,000-strong security deployment for the 2026 FIFA World Cup — with Guadalajara, the heart of CJNG territory, under particular scrutiny. The Trump administration granted Mexico a 90-day tariff postponement as bilateral security cooperation continues, with 55+ high-value cartel figures transferred to U.S. custody. No senior-command-level CJNG captures were announced in the March 25–28 period.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera, UPI, Excélsior, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, Janes, Chatham House, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: March 28, 2026.
